sections.paragraph:
ξηρός, ά, όν (Hipponax [VI b.c.]; Hdt.+; ins, pap, LXX, En; TestSol 10:6 C; TestZeb 2:7; JosAs 13:8; Philo; Jos., Bell. 3, 228, Ant. 5, 249; Just., D. 117, 3; 131, 3; Mel.; Hs 8, 4, 6 adj. with two endings?)

① pert. to total loss of moisture, dry, dried up, lit., of ξύλον (q.v. 3 and GDI 4689, 108 [Messenia] ξηρὰ ξύλα; PEdg 93 [=Sb 6808], 1 [256 b.c.]; Is 56:3; Ezk 17:24) Lk 23:31; Hs 4:4. Of trees (Lucian, Sat. 9) 3:1ff; 4:1, 4. Of branches (POxy 1188, 4 [13 a.d.]; Kaibel 1039, 14) 8, 1, 6f and 11f; 8, 2, 6; 8, 4, 4ff; 8, 5, 2ff; 8, 6, 4f; 8, 8, 1 and 4; 8, 9, 1; 8, 10, 1 and 3. Of plants 9, 1, 6; 9, 21, 1; hence also in imagery θεμέλια (corresp. to ῥίζαι) 9, 21, 2 and even of pers.: δίψυχοι ibid.; cp. 4:4. Of seeds 1 Cl 24:5. ἡ ξ. γῆ dry land Hb 11:29. Also simply ἡ ξηρά the dry (land, ground) (X., Oec. 17, 2; 19, 7; Aristot., HA 5, 10; Gen 1:9 al. in LXX) Hb 11:29 v.l.; Hv 3, 2, 7; 3, 5, 3. W. θάλασσα (Jon 1:9; Hg 2:21; 1 Macc 8:23, 32; En 97:7) Mt 23:15.

② pert. to being shrunken or withered and therefore immobile because of disease, withered, shrunken, paralyzed, fig. ext. of 1 (cp. ξηραίνω 2; ξηρότης Galen VII 666, 1 K.=a wasting disease.—ξηρός in this sense on the third stele of Epidaurus ln. 108 as read by RHerzog, D. Wunderheilungen v. Ep. 1931, 32 and 138. ἡμίξηρος=half-stiffened Hippiatr. I 185, 9; χεὶρ ἡμίξηρος TestSim 2, 12. Cp. also Hipponax 10 West [30 Degani; 11 Diehl3] λιμῷ γένηται [Diehl accepts opt. γένοιτο against codd.] ξηρός; Hos 9:14 μαστοὶ ξηροί; Psellus p. 27, 17 νηδὺς ξηρά of the womb of an aged woman) χεὶρ ξηρά a withered hand Mt 12:10; Mk 3:3; Lk 6:6, 8 (Mel., P. 78, 562; 90, 668). ξηροί withered, paralyzed (Lucian, Tox. 24 of a woman ξηρὰ τὸ ἥμισυ) J 5:3.—On the mng. of the word DHesseling, Sertum Nabericum 1908, 145–54; Renehan ’75, 145f.—B. 1076. DELG. M-M.

Hipponax Hipponax , VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

adj. adj. = adjective

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

GDI GDI = Sammlung der griechischen Dialekt-Inschriften—List 3

PEdg PEdg = CDE., Selected Papyri—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

corresp. corresp. = corresponding(ly)

pers. pers. = person(s)

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Hv Hv = Visions

ext. ext. = extension

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

  1. ln. = line

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Hippiatr. Hippiatr. = Corpus hippiatricorum Graecorum, IX a.d.—List 5

TestSim TestSim = Testament of Simeon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

West West = MWest, Iambi et Elegi Graeci—List 5

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

opt. opt. = optative

Psellus Psellus , History, XI a.d.—List 5

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Renehan Renehan = RR., Greek Lexicographical Notes—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 685
